source.document: FIDIC Conditions of Contract for Construction

clauses.title: Priority of Documents
clauses.engineers_action: Resolve ambiguities or discrepancies by applying document priority order
clauses.time_limit: None specified
clauses.key_principle: Priority: (1) Contract Agreement, (2) Letter of Acceptance, (3) Letter of Tender, (4) Particular Conditions, (5) General Conditions, (6) Specification, (7) Drawings, (8) Schedules
clauses.common_pitfall: Applying General Conditions when Particular Conditions have modified the provision

clauses.title: Right of Access to the Site
clauses.engineers_action: Monitor Employer's obligation to give right of access. Document any delays.
clauses.time_limit: Commencement Date (or as stated in Particular Conditions)
clauses.key_principle: Employer shall give right of access to and possession of the Site. Failure gives Contractor entitlement to Cost + profit and time extension.
clauses.common_pitfall: Not documenting delayed access as a potential Contractor entitlement

clauses.title: Determinations
clauses.engineers_action: Make fair determination of any matter under the Contract
clauses.time_limit: 42 days from notification (or as modified by Particular Conditions)
clauses.key_principle: Engineer shall consult each Party, endeavour to reach agreement. If not, make a fair determination in accordance with the Contract. This is an impartial function.
clauses.common_pitfall: Acting as Employer's agent rather than impartial certifier

clauses.title: Unforeseeable Physical Conditions
clauses.engineers_action: Assess whether physical conditions were unforeseeable by an experienced contractor
clauses.time_limit: Contractor must notify promptly; 28-day claim notice per 20.1
clauses.key_principle: If conditions are unforeseeable, Contractor entitled to Cost (not profit). Engineer inspects and assesses. If conditions could have been foreseen, no entitlement.
clauses.common_pitfall: Confusing 'unforeseeable' with 'unforeseen' -- the test is what an experienced contractor should have anticipated

clauses.title: Commencement of Works
clauses.engineers_action: Issue notice to commence. Verify Contractor commences within 28 days.
clauses.time_limit: Contractor commences within 28 days after receiving notice
clauses.key_principle: Engineer gives notice when Contractor has provided Performance Security and evidence of insurance.
clauses.common_pitfall: Issuing commencement notice before Performance Security is in place

clauses.title: Time for Completion
clauses.engineers_action: Monitor progress against Time for Completion stated in Appendix to Tender
clauses.time_limit: As stated in Appendix to Tender
clauses.key_principle: Time for Completion is the number of days from Commencement Date. Extensions granted under Sub-Clause 8.4 are added.
clauses.common_pitfall: Not updating the completion date when extensions are granted

clauses.title: Extension of Time for Completion
clauses.engineers_action: Assess entitlement to extension. Determine fair extension under Sub-Clause 3.5.
clauses.time_limit: Contractor must claim per Sub-Clause 20.1 (28-day notice)
clauses.key_principle: Extension available for: (a) Variation/quantity change, (b) entitlement under other clause, (c) exceptionally adverse climatic conditions, (d) unforeseeable shortages, (e) Employer-caused delay.
clauses.common_pitfall: Granting extension for normal seasonal weather (not 'exceptionally adverse')

clauses.title: Delay Damages
clauses.engineers_action: Certify delay damages if Contractor fails to complete on time
clauses.time_limit: From expiry of Time for Completion (as extended)
clauses.key_principle: Delay damages at rate stated in Appendix to Tender. Subject to maximum stated in Appendix. Employer may deduct from amounts due.
clauses.common_pitfall: Applying delay damages before granting entitled time extensions

clauses.title: Taking Over of the Works and Sections
clauses.engineers_action: Issue Taking-Over Certificate when Works are complete (or substantially complete)
clauses.time_limit: Within 28 days of receiving Contractor's application (or deemed if Engineer fails)
clauses.key_principle: Works considered complete when tests on completion passed and only minor outstanding work remains. Taking-Over triggers retention release (first half) and starts Defects Notification Period.
clauses.common_pitfall: Refusing to issue TOC for minor defects that do not prevent use of the Works

clauses.title: Completion of Outstanding Work and Remedying Defects
clauses.engineers_action: Issue notices of defects during Defects Notification Period
clauses.time_limit: Defects Notification Period (stated in Appendix to Tender, typically 365 days)
clauses.key_principle: Contractor shall complete outstanding work and remedy defects during the DNP. Engineer may extend DNP if defects or damage cannot be remedied expeditiously (Sub-Clause 11.3).
clauses.common_pitfall: Not issuing defects notices in writing within the DNP

clauses.title: Right to Vary
clauses.engineers_action: Initiate Variation by instruction or request proposal from Contractor
clauses.time_limit: None specified for instruction; Contractor's proposal timing per 13.3
clauses.key_principle: Engineer may instruct variations at any time before Taking-Over Certificate. Variations may include changes to quantities, quality, dimensions, levels, sequence, timing, or methods.
clauses.common_pitfall: Instructing changes that fundamentally alter the nature of the Works (may not be a valid Variation)

clauses.title: Evaluation
clauses.engineers_action: Agree or determine rates for varied work
clauses.time_limit: None specified; typically before next IPC
clauses.key_principle: Use contract rates where applicable. If not applicable, derive new rate from contract rates or agree new rate. If cannot agree, Engineer determines per Sub-Clause 3.5.
clauses.common_pitfall: Using contract rates for work that is fundamentally different in character from the original item

clauses.title: Value Engineering
clauses.engineers_action: Evaluate Contractor's proposal for value engineering savings
clauses.time_limit: None specified
clauses.key_principle: Contractor may propose changes that reduce cost or improve value. If accepted, savings shared (typically 50/50 unless Particular Conditions specify otherwise).
clauses.common_pitfall: Accepting value engineering proposals without properly assessing long-term performance implications

clauses.title: Application for Interim Payment Certificate
clauses.engineers_action: Receive and verify Contractor's monthly Statement
clauses.time_limit: Contractor submits monthly (or as stated in Particular Conditions)
clauses.key_principle: Statement includes estimated contract value of work done, plus amounts the Contractor considers due. Supporting documents required.
clauses.common_pitfall: Rejecting a Statement for minor omissions instead of certifying the verified amount

clauses.title: Issue of Interim Payment Certificates
clauses.engineers_action: Issue IPC within 28 days of receiving Statement
clauses.time_limit: 28 days after receiving Contractor's Statement
clauses.key_principle: Engineer certifies the amount considered correct. May differ from Contractor's claimed amount. If Engineer fails to issue, Contractor has grounds under Sub-Clause 16.1.
clauses.common_pitfall: Failing to issue IPC within 28 days -- gives Contractor right to suspend

clauses.title: Payment
clauses.engineers_action: Monitor Employer's payment within 56 days of Statement receipt
clauses.time_limit: Employer pays within 56 days after receiving the Statement
clauses.key_principle: Payment is the Employer's obligation, not the Engineer's. But the Engineer should monitor and advise on late payment consequences.
clauses.common_pitfall: Not documenting late payments and their entitlement consequences

clauses.title: Termination by Employer
clauses.engineers_action: Advise Employer on grounds for termination and correct procedure
clauses.time_limit: 14-day notice (or as modified by Particular Conditions)
clauses.key_principle: Employer may terminate if Contractor: fails to provide security (4.2), abandons works, subcontracts whole works, is bankrupt, or offers bribe. Termination must follow correct notice procedure.
clauses.common_pitfall: Employer terminating without following the notice procedure -- creates counter-claims

clauses.title: Contractor's Entitlement to Suspend Work
clauses.engineers_action: Recognise Contractor's right to suspend if Engineer fails to certify or Employer fails to pay
clauses.time_limit: 21-day notice before suspension
clauses.key_principle: Contractor may suspend if: (a) Engineer fails to issue IPC per 14.6, or (b) Employer fails to comply with Sub-Clause 2.4 (financial arrangements), or (c) Employer fails to pay. 21-day notice required before suspension.
clauses.common_pitfall: Not recognising the Contractor's legal right to suspend -- attempting to impose delay damages during a valid suspension

clauses.title: Definition of Force Majeure
clauses.engineers_action: Assess whether claimed event meets all four elements of the definition
clauses.time_limit: Affected Party gives notice within 14 days (19.2)
clauses.key_principle: Four elements: (1) beyond Party's control, (2) could not reasonably have been provided against before contract, (3) could not reasonably have been avoided or overcome, (4) not substantially attributable to the other Party.
clauses.common_pitfall: Accepting seasonal events (rainy season, normal flooding) as force majeure when they are foreseeable

clauses.title: Contractor's Claims
clauses.engineers_action: Check compliance with 28-day notice requirement. Assess claim.
clauses.time_limit: 28-day notice bar. Detailed particulars within 42 days. Engineer responds within 42 days.
clauses.key_principle: If Contractor fails to give notice within 28 days: no entitlement, Employer discharged from liability. This is the most critical procedural requirement. The time bar is strict unless modified by Particular Conditions.
clauses.common_pitfall: Not applying the time bar when notice is late -- or applying it without checking Particular Conditions

clauses.title: Obtaining Dispute Adjudication Board's Decision
clauses.engineers_action: Cooperate with DAB process. Provide records and determinations as required.
clauses.time_limit: DAB decision within 84 days. Notice of dissatisfaction within 28 days.
clauses.key_principle: Either Party may refer a dispute to the DAB. DAB gives decision within 84 days. Decision is binding unless either Party gives notice of dissatisfaction within 28 days.
clauses.common_pitfall: Missing the 28-day notice of dissatisfaction -- the DAB decision becomes final and binding
